summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 7 (ENPH-FR)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: RAS-targeting biotech: dated binary ERAS-4001 first efficacy/safety readout H2-2026; de-risked ($409M cash into H2-28, $225M raise) + naporafenib killed to focus franchise, but early-stage and asymmetry not clearly mispriced.
LIVE merger-arb: ARCHIMED to acquire at $3.16/sh cash + up to $100M CVR (signed May 1'26, ~58% premium); shareholder vote pending, no financing condition, expected close Q3'26.
DURAVYU wet-AMD Ph3 binaries: LUGANO topline ~Aug-2026, LUCIA ~Oct-2026 (non-inferiority vs Eylea); DSMC cleared 3rd review — single-asset dated binary with real asymmetry.

May 27 2026 launched FORMAL strategic review of Fiberon decking (potential divestiture/separation) + activist Ed Garden (Trian) buying; stock down ~24%. Real activist+structural trigger, process forward/not fired, outcome undated -> WATCH.
Extended lock-up on ~54.1% of Class A shares expires Aug 31 2026 — a dated forward supply-overhang event (own the suppressed underlying once it clears); standard 180d already passed Jan 27.

Definitive Apr-30-2026 deal to sell Long Ridge to MARA for $1.512B; proceeds wipe $1.16B asset debt + ~$300M parent debt; dated Q3-2026 close gated on FERC/HSR/STB — own the de-levering RemainCo (freight rail).

Starboard ~5% pushing value-unlock; Fluor converting+monetizing remaining ~40% NuScale stake, completion targeted by end-Q2-2026 — dated overhang-clearing/re-rate catalyst (Fluor chose monetization over the cleaner tax-free spin Starboard wanted).

Board on Feb-4-2026 authorized exploration of strategic options 'including but not limited to a sale of the company' — genuine dated review, but explicitly preliminary with no assurance; Rynaxypyr post-patent overhang is the offset.

verified.catalyst: Erasca's first-in-human pan-KRAS inhibitor ERAS-4001 (BOREALIS-1 Phase 1) has its initial monotherapy readout — topline safety, tolerability, PK, and first efficacy in KRAS-mutant solid tumors — a dated, forward, unfired clinical binary.
verified.dated_milestone: ERAS-4001 initial Phase 1 monotherapy data (safety/PK/initial efficacy), planned H2 2026; preceded by the more pivotal ERAS-0015 (FLAGSHP-1) initial Phase 1 data in H1 2026 (early data already shown Jan 12, 2026).
verified.instrument: equity (NASDAQ: ERAS, ~$13.88, ~$4.3B mcap as of 2026-06-04); pre-binary long-dated calls if implied vol affordable into H2-2026
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific dated forward figure: H2-2026 first ERAS-4001 efficacy/safety readout), partial — but the figure is NOT clearly unpriced
verified.edge: L — the dominant valuation driver (pan-RAS validation) is already substantially re-rated; AInvest frames the ~$4.5B valuation as "hinging on pan-RAS validation," and ERAS-0015 early data was already disclosed Jan 12, 2026. ERAS-4001's first cut is early-stage, small-N, safety/PK-weighted; asymmetry not demonstrably mispriced.
verified.primary_source: Erasca PR "Promising Early Clinical Data for ERAS-0015 and 2026-2027 Milestones" (Jan 12, 2026); cash/runway via StockTitan/GlobeNewswire (Jan 2026, $408.5M into H2-2028); naporafenib termination via FierceBiotech (May 2025); price/mcap via stockanalysis.com (2026-06-04, ~$13.88/$4.3B)
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, dated, and forward: ERAS-4001's initial Phase 1 monotherapy readout (safety/tolerability/PK/initial efficacy) is guided to H2 2026 per Erasca's Jan 12, 2026 release, and the company is genuinely de-risked — a Jan-2026 raise (~$242.7M net) lifted cash to $408.5M with runway into H2 2028, while naporafenib was discontinued (Novartis license terminated eff. June 3, 2026) to concentrate on the RAS franchise. However, the asymmetry is weak: the more pivotal near-term event is ERAS-0015 H1-2026 data (already partially shown), the pan-RAS thesis is largely in the ~$4.3B market cap, and ERAS-4001's first readout is early/dose-escalation with small N and a safety-weighted first look. Holds WATCH/5 — concrete dated milestone but no clearly-mispriced figure (G3 only partial) and low edge.

verified.catalyst: ARCHIMED (via Essence Parent Inc.) to acquire Esperion for $3.16/sh cash plus one non-tradeable CVR worth up to $100M ($40M bempedoic-acid + $60M enbumyst U.S.-sales milestones); definitive merger agreement signed May 1, 2026 at a 58% premium, no financing condition, expected to close Q3 2026 subject to shareholder vote.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder special meeting / vote (date still TBD — PREM14A filed May 29, 2026 has meeting date in brackets, no DEFM14A yet); expected close Q3 2026. Next hard catalyst = DEFM14A filing setting the vote date.
verified.instrument: stock (cash-merger convergence) + CVR optionality; the residual edge is arb spread, not a re-rating long
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (ARCHIMED named acquirer), G2 (signed May 1'26 merger agreement, $3.16 cash, no financing out), G3 (up to $100M CVR currently unpriced)
verified.edge: L — the +58% announcement re-rate already FIRED May 1; stock at $3.13 vs $3.16 cash floor leaves only ~1% (~$0.03) gross convergence spread. Forward edge is the unpriced CVR (up to ~$0.49/sh, but contingent/non-tradeable and back-end-loaded on sales milestones) plus modest spread capture into a Q3 close.
verified.primary_source: Esperion press release "Esperion to be Acquired by ARCHIMED" + Form 8-K, May 1, 2026 (globenewswire / esperion.com); PREM14A filed May 29, 2026 (SEC EDGAR CIK 0001434868); FMP quote $3.13 as of 2026-06-06.
verified.analysis: Catalyst is real, specific, and idiosyncratic (clears G1/G2/G3): ARCHIMED's $3.16/sh all-cash buyout of ESPR is a signed deal with no financing condition, so deal-completion risk is the main residual variable. However the primary value event already FIRED — the stock re-rated +58% on the May 1 announcement and now trades at $3.13, a hair under the $3.16 cash floor, so the remaining forward payoff is a thin ~1% arb spread plus contingent CVR optionality, not a fresh upside catalyst. The dated milestone is soft: the vote date is not yet set (PREM14A still bracketed), so this is WATCH/score 6 — credible arb to monitor for the DEFM14A vote date and any topping bid, but the convergence return is low and the big move is behind it.

verified.catalyst: EyePoint's fully-enrolled pivotal Phase 3 wet-AMD readouts for DURAVYU (vorolanib/Durasert E) — LUGANO topline targeted mid-2026 with LUCIA (non-inferiority vs aflibercept/Eylea on avg BCVA change at wks 52/56) to follow shortly after — a single-asset binary for a ~$1.04B-cap name.
verified.dated_milestone: LUGANO Phase 3 wet-AMD topline targeted mid-2026 (next milestone, ~within 1-3 months); LUCIA topline "to follow shortly after." Last gate cleared: 3rd consecutive positive DSMC review on 2026-05-14 (no protocol changes).
verified.instrument: equity (small-cap single-asset binary; long-dated calls straddling the readout window viable given clean dated event)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (imminent binary Phase 3 efficacy outcome the market cannot yet price), with G2 support (concrete committed milestone — both trials fully enrolled, dated readout)
verified.edge: M — clean dated single-asset binary with genuine asymmetry and an unpriced outcome, but the event is well-telegraphed (sell-side Buy ratings, DSMC PR moved the tape), so it is known rather than overlooked; edge is in the binary distribution/IV, not in surprise.
verified.primary_source: EyePoint press release via StockTitan/GlobeNewswire, 2026-05-14 ("Third Consecutive Positive DSMC Recommendation... Building Confidence Ahead of Mid-2026 Topline Data"); enrollment-complete PR 2025-07-29. Stock ~$12.38 / mkt cap ~$1.04B per Nasdaq/stockanalysis, 2026-06-03.
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources (EyePoint SEC 8-K / GlobeNewswire, May 14 2026) confirm every load-bearing fact: DURAVYU (vorolanib + Durasert E) in two fully-enrolled (>900 pts, 1:1 vs aflibercept/Eylea) pivotal Phase 3 wet-AMD trials, LUGANO + LUCIA; endpoint is verbatim 'non-inferiority in the average change in BCVA at weeks 52 and 56' vs aflibercept; LUGANO topline guided 'mid-2026' with LUCIA 'to follow shortly after'; the May-14-2026 3rd consecutive positive DSMC review is a masked SAFETY/continuation checkpoint, NOT the efficacy topline, so the single-asset binary remains forward and unpriced; ~$1.04B cap (px ~$12.34, Jun-4-2026), DURAVYU is the lead/single asset. Forward, not fired. Sole correction: the triage's exact 'LUGANO ~Aug / LUCIA ~Oct' months are NOT company-confirmed (official guidance is only 'mid-2026' + 'shortly after'), a timing-precision caveat the triage already disclosed; a slip into 2H-2026 is possible.
verified.analysis: Verified real and specific: named program (DURAVYU), named comparator (Eylea/aflibercept), two named fully-enrolled (>900 pts) pivotal trials, concrete non-inferiority BCVA endpoint, and a dated forward readout (LUGANO mid-2026, LUCIA shortly after) — a classic single-asset dated binary for a sub-$1.1B-cap, not fired and not a macro bet. The May-14-2026 3rd-consecutive positive DSMC review is a de-risking checkpoint (safety/futility), NOT the efficacy topline, so the binary remains forward and unpriced. Gap/caveat: the triage's exact "LUGANO ~Aug-2026 / LUCIA ~Oct-2026" months are NOT company-confirmed — official guidance is only "mid-2026" + "shortly after," so the precise calendar date carries timing uncertainty (a slip into 2H-2026 is possible); confirm the hard readout date nearer the event.

verified.catalyst: Fortune Brands launched a formal, board-backed strategic review of its Fiberon composite-decking business (sale or spin-off) on May 27 2026, with activist Ed Garden (Trian co-founder, now a director) buying ~$113M of stock May 29 after a ~24% decline.
verified.dated_milestone: No committed date — company explicitly set NO timetable for the review; next natural read is Q2 2026 earnings (~late July 2026), unconfirmed as an update point.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 partial (named director/activist Ed Garden, but no named Fiberon counterparty); G3 partial (separable unit, ~$470M 2018 acquisition cost, Outdoors ~30% of sales — but no firm currently-unpriced figure and shares already re-rated down ~24%).
verified.edge: L — real structural setup but the review + activist buy are public and the ~24% drop has already re-priced the name; no dated milestone to trade against.
verified.primary_source: Fortune Brands Business Wire press release + SEC Form 8-K (Ex-99.1), "Initiates Strategic Review of Fiberon," May 27 2026; Motley Fool, "Stock Is Down 24%... One Investor Bought $113 Million," May 29 2026.
verified.analysis: This is a genuine activist+structural trigger: a formal strategic review (potential divestiture/separation) of Fiberon with Trian's Ed Garden on the board and adding ~$113M, confirmed via Business Wire and the SEC 8-K dated May 27 2026. However it fails the DATED gate outright — Fortune Brands explicitly stated it has set no timetable and gives no assurance of any transaction or outcome — and the catalyst has already fired into a ~24% decline, so it is not a clean unpriced forward event. Correctly WATCH, not ACTIVE: monitor for a definitive sale/spin-off agreement (named buyer or terms) or a dated update at Q2 earnings, which would re-rate it to a tradable catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Final 35% tranche (~77.7M Class A shares) of Figma's Extended Lock-Up clears on the earlier of Q2-2026 earnings or Aug 31 2026, removing the last leg of the 54.1% supply overhang.
verified.dated_milestone: ~Aug 20 2026 (2nd trading day after Q2-2026 earnings, scheduled Aug 18 2026), hard-capped Aug 31 2026 — the operative date, NOT Aug 31 as triage stated.
verified.instrument: stock (own suppressed underlying into/through final unlock; supply-clearing mean-reversion, not a positive re-rate)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 partial-FAIL: the 77.7M-share final tranche is a specific dated figure but is NOT currently-unpriced (disclosed in every prospectus/8-K/analyst note since Sep 3 2025); no G1 counterparty, no G2 commitment.
verified.edge: L — maximally telegraphed; FIG fell ~81% partly on these exact lock-up mechanics, so overhang is already re-rated/priced. Edge only if the final 35% clears with a price pop on supply removal, which is a low-conviction technical bet.
verified.primary_source: SEC 8-K Figma Inc., filed Sep 3 2025 (fig-20250903) re: Extended Lock-Up tranche schedule; Q1'26 earnings 8-K/press release May 14 2026; Q2'26 earnings scheduled Aug 18 2026.
verified.analysis: Claim is factually accurate on coverage (54.1% Class A, Extended Lock-Up dated Aug 30 2025 / 8-K Sep 3 2025) but materially mischaracterizes status and date: the lock-up releases in tranches (17.5% Q3'25 + 20% FY'25 + 27.5% at Q1'26 earnings, which FIRED May 14 2026), so ~62.5% has already cleared and only the final 35% (~77.7M) remains. The remaining tranche's operative trigger is the EARLIER of Q2'26 earnings (Aug 18 2026 -> ~Aug 20) or Aug 31 2026, so the true date is ~Aug 20, not Aug 31. It is dated and forward but heavily priced (no unpriced G3 figure) and is a supply-overhang clearing, not a discrete positive catalyst — keeps WATCH, fails the strict ACTIVE bar.

verified.catalyst: FTAI Infrastructure's definitive Apr-29/30-2026 sale of Long Ridge Energy & Power to MARA Holdings for $1.512B eliminates $1.16B subsidiary debt plus ~$300M parent debt, repairing the balance sheet of a ~$490M-mcap company and leaving a de-levered freight-rail/terminals RemainCo.
verified.dated_milestone: Deal close expected Q3-2026, gated on FERC change-of-control (mgmt guided ~middle of Q3), HSR, and STB rail coordination; next catalyst = FERC approval ~mid-Q3-2026.
verified.instrument: equity (FIP common); event-driven de-levering — long stock into close, optionally Q3/Q4-2026 calls to capture the re-rating on debt removal
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty MARA Holdings) + G2 (concrete $1.512B definitive agreement w/ $20M escrow) + G3 ($1.46B total debt elimination unpriced vs ~$490M mcap; stock near 52wk-low, sold off post-announce, not re-rated)
verified.edge: H — $1.46B debt removal vs ~$490M mcap is a transformational, quantified balance-sheet event; FIP trades $4.16 (52wk-low $3.90, vs 200d $5.12), so the de-levering is clearly NOT in the price. Edge tempered by binary FERC/HSR/STB regulatory gate and the cash-flow loss of the only profitable asset, but the deleveraging thesis on RemainCo is intact.
verified.primary_source: FTAI Infrastructure 8-K / press release "Announces Agreement to Sell Long Ridge Energy and Power to MARA Holdings" (Apr 30, 2026, ir.fipinc.com & SEC EDGAR CIK 0001899883); MARA IR release (Apr 30, 2026); FIP quote $4.16 (FMP, 2026-06-05 close)
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed vs PRIMARY sources (FTAI IR press release, MARA IR/8-K, SEC 8-K via StockTitan, GlobeNewswire, TradingView): definitive agreement announced Apr 29-30 2026 to sell Long Ridge Energy & Power to a MARA Holdings subsidiary for $1.512B; eliminates $1.16B Long Ridge debt + repays ~$300M parent debt (~$30M/yr interest savings); expected close Q3/2H 2026 subject to FERC/HSR/STB. Forward and NOT terminated as of June 6 2026 -- in fact de-risked: bondholder consent solicitation expired May 15 2026 with consents secured, avoiding the 101% change-of-control put (per FIP Q1 transcript June 1 2026). Tradeable: mcap ~$491.56M (June 5 2026 close $4.16), matching the line's ~$490M. Stock sits near 52-wk low ($3.90; range to $7.935), confirming it sold off rather than re-rated -> de-levering of freight-rail/terminals RemainCo (Transtar + Wheeling & Lake Erie, $40.2M Q1 adj EBITDA; Repauno/Jefferson) is unpriced. Genuinely idiosyncratic event-driven, not macro. Real binary risk = the regulatory gate, and Long Ridge was a cash-flow contributor being sold -- both flagged accurately in the line.
verified.analysis: Definitive deal confirmed via FTAI IR, MARA IR, SEC 8-K and TradingView (Apr 29-30, 2026): $1.512B base price wiping $1.16B Long Ridge + ~$300M parent debt, expected to close Q3-2026 on FERC/HSR/STB approval. As of early June 2026 the deal is signed but unclosed and not terminated, so it is genuinely forward; FIP has sold off to near its 52-week low rather than re-rating, meaning the de-levering of the freight-rail/terminals RemainCo is unpriced. Real, named, specific, dated, forward and idiosyncratic — not a macro bet; main risk is the binary regulatory gate and that Long Ridge was the cash-flow engine being sold.

verified.catalyst: Fluor's monetization of its remaining ~40% NuScale stake (the Starboard-driven value-unlock/overhang-clearing event) that the WATCH flag targeted for end-Q2-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: FIRED — final 40M NuScale shares sold and full exit completed April 23, 2026; no dated forward milestone remains (residual $1.4B 2026 buyback has ~$884M left but no specific completion date).
verified.instrument: none (catalyst already realized; equity already re-rated)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 was met at flag time (concrete commitment to convert+monetize, completion targeted end-Q2-2026) but the commitment has since been executed/completed, so no live gate holds now.
verified.edge: L — the overhang is fully cleared and the re-rate is already in the price (+71% total return by Feb 2026; +326% realized on the position), so the asymmetric setup is gone.
verified.primary_source: Fluor newsroom "Fluor Receives $1.35B for 71M NuScale Shares; Launches Trading Program for Remaining 40M Shares" + TipRanks/Globe&Mail "Fluor Completes Monetization of NuScale Investment Stake" (completion April 23, 2026); Motley Fool FLR Q1 2026 earnings transcript (June 1, 2026).
verified.analysis: The flagged catalyst — Fluor monetizing its remaining ~40% NuScale stake to clear the overhang Starboard targeted — is FIRED, not forward: Fluor sold the final 40M shares for $473M and completed the full exit on April 23, 2026 (~$2.43B total proceeds, 4.5x MOIC), roughly six weeks before this verification. The re-rate has already played out (stock +71% total return by Feb 2026), and management on the June 1 Q1 call gave no sum-of-parts/discount commentary and no new dated value-unlock milestone. The only residual leg is the $1.4B 2026 buyback (~$884M remaining) which is an undated capital-return program, not a discrete priced catalyst; near-term risk is now macro (Middle East/geopolitics affecting guidance), which is explicitly out of scope.

verified.catalyst: FMC's board authorized (Feb-4-2026) exploration of strategic options "including but not limited to a sale of the company," and management committed on the Q1 call to deliver more clarity on that review by end of Q2 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Strategic-review update promised "by end of Q2 2026," to be communicated on the Q2 2026 earnings call (late Jul / early Aug 2026); India commercial-business sale targeted to sign May 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (long stock); de-rating to 52-wk low favors a defined-risk call-spread into the Q2 update for the binary
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (board authorization + management's explicit, dated commitment to a strategic-options update by end-Q2 2026) and partially G3 (analyst consensus ~$19 vs $11.64 spot, ~55% below est. fair value — optionality is de-rated/unpriced). G1 fails: no named whole-company acquirer (India sub-sale has unnamed "several buyers").
verified.edge: M — real, dated, near-term binary (Q2 update ~6-8 wks out) with a large valuation gap and stock at 52-wk lows (optionality faded, not priced), but no named counterparty and a genuine Rynaxypyr post-patent / weak-interest-coverage overhang cap conviction.
verified.primary_source: FMC Q1 2026 earnings call (early May 2026, via Investing.com/GuruFocus recaps): "by the end of Q2 [we] expect to have more clarity on a review of strategic options" — and FMC press release "FMC sets 2026 priorities and announces exploration of strategic options including but not limited to the sale of the company," Feb-4-2026 (PRNewswire/investors.fmc.com); spot $11.64 via FMP quote (2026-06-05).
verified.analysis: The Feb-4-2026 strategic review is genuine, specific and dated, and remains forward — not fired (no transaction) and the reverse of re-rated: FMC trades $11.64 at its 52-week low, below the Feb-announcement $13.67 and the post-Q1 $16.40 spike and far under its 50d/200d ($15.08/$19.73), so the market has faded the sale optionality. The hard catalyst is management's commitment to update the strategic-options review by end of Q2 2026 (Q2 earnings call), creating a defined binary with ~55-63% upside to consensus/fair-value if a credible bidder or deal emerges. It stays WATCH not ACTIVE because the process is explicitly preliminary with "no assurance," there is no named acquirer, and the Rynaxypyr post-patent revenue erosion (FY guide -5%, mid-single-digit price decline) plus weak interest coverage are a real fundamental offset and the reason for the de-rating.
